description: Deep-dive research and CUDA implementation of TurboQuant for GLM5 and DeepSeekV3.2 KV cache quantization

researchGuidelines.targetAudience: Someone with Calculus 3 and Linear Algebra background who may need refreshers on specific concepts
researchGuidelines.mathFormatting: All math must be formatted in LaTeX within Markdown (inline: $...$ , display: $$...$$)
researchGuidelines.comprehensiveness: Thorough coverage without verbosity. Every claim should be justified. Include refreshers on prerequisite concepts when needed.
researchGuidelines.sources: Cite all web sources with URLs. For local code analysis, reference specific file paths.
researchGuidelines.outputFormat: Markdown files in research/ directory with proper LaTeX math rendering

userStories.title: Research Shannon's Source Coding Theory
userStories.description: Understand Shannon's foundational work on lossy compression and distortion-rate functions. Write for someone with Calc 3 + Linear Algebra who needs concept refreshers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/01-shannon-source-coding.md with LaTeX-formatted math
Include refresher: What is entropy? What is information content?
Explain distortion-rate function D(R) with intuitive buildup
Document Shannon Lower Bound (Lemma 2 from paper) with step-by-step derivation
Include worked numerical example showing the bound in action
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research Beta Distribution on Hyperspheres
userStories.description: Understand why random rotation induces Beta distribution on coordinates. Build intuition from Linear Algebra foundations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/02-hypersphere-distributions.md with LaTeX math
Refresher: What is an orthogonal matrix? Why does Qx preserve ||x||?
Refresher: What is a hypersphere S^(d-1)? Visualize for d=2,3
Explain Gamma function Γ(z) with examples: Γ(1)=1, Γ(1/2)=√π, Γ(n)=(n-1)!
Show convergence to N(0, 1/d) for large d with numerical examples
Explain concentration of measure intuitively
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research Lloyd-Max Scalar Quantization
userStories.description: Understand optimal scalar quantization and codebook construction as a continuous k-means problem.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/03-lloyd-max-quantization.md with LaTeX math
Refresher: What is k-means clustering? What is the objective?
Explain quantization as partitioning the real line into intervals
Derive the k-means formulation (Equation 4 from paper)
Explain Voronoi tessellation: why boundaries are midpoints between centroids
Show the Lloyd-Max iterative algorithm step-by-step
Compute optimal codebooks for b=1,2,3,4 bits (include actual numerical values)
Explain Panter-Dite high-resolution formula with derivation sketch
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research QJL Transform
userStories.description: Understand Quantized Johnson-Lindenstrauss for 1-bit inner product quantization.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/04-qjl-transform.md with LaTeX math
Refresher: What is an inner product ⟨x,y⟩? Geometric interpretation
Refresher: What is the Johnson-Lindenstrauss lemma? Why does random projection preserve distances?
Document Definition 1: Qqjl(x) = sign(S·x) where S has i.i.d. N(0,1) entries
Prove unbiasedness: E[⟨y, Q^(-1)(Q(x))⟩] = ⟨y,x⟩ step-by-step
Derive variance bound: Var ≤ (π/2d)·||y||²
Explain why sign() preserves inner product information (intuition + math)
Include reference to QJL paper (arXiv:2406.03482)
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Research MSE vs Inner Product Bias Problem
userStories.description: Understand why MSE-optimal quantizers are biased for inner products and how the two-stage approach fixes this.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/05-mse-vs-inner-product-bias.md with LaTeX math
Refresher: What does 'unbiased estimator' mean? E[θ̂] = θ
Work through the 1-bit example completely: show E[⟨y, Q^(-1)(Q(x))⟩] = (2/π)·⟨y,x⟩
Explain where the 2/π factor comes from geometrically
Show how bias decreases with higher bit-widths (table of bias factors)
Explain the two-stage solution: MSE(b-1) + QJL(1) on residual
Prove why this combination is unbiased
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Document TurboQuant_mse Algorithm
userStories.description: Fully document Algorithm 1 (MSE-optimized TurboQuant) with annotated pseudocode.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/06-turboquant-mse-algorithm.md with LaTeX math
Annotated pseudocode for Algorithm 1 with explanation of each line
Explain rotation matrix generation via QR decomposition
Refresher: What is QR decomposition? Why does it give orthogonal Q?
Document Quant_mse procedure: rotate → find nearest centroids → store indices
Document DeQuant_mse procedure: lookup centroids → rotate back
Walk through Theorem 1 proof (distortion bound) step-by-step
Include worked example: quantize a specific vector x with b=2 bits
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Document TurboQuant_prod Algorithm
userStories.description: Fully document Algorithm 2 (inner-product-optimized TurboQuant) with annotated pseudocode.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/07-turboquant-prod-algorithm.md with LaTeX math
Annotated pseudocode for Algorithm 2 with explanation of each line
Explain the two-stage structure: why use (b-1) bits for MSE and 1 bit for QJL
Document residual computation: r = x - Q^(-1)_mse(Q_mse(x))
Explain what is stored: (idx, qjl, ||r||) and why we need the norm
Document Quant_prod and DeQuant_prod procedures
Walk through Theorem 2 proof (unbiasedness + distortion bound)
Include worked example: quantize a vector and verify unbiasedness numerically
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Document Lower Bounds and Optimality
userStories.description: Understand information-theoretic lower bounds and verify TurboQuant's near-optimality.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/08-lower-bounds-optimality.md with LaTeX math
Refresher: What is Yao's minimax principle? Relate randomized algorithms to distributions
Document Theorem 3 (lower bounds) with full proof
Show the connection to Shannon Lower Bound from US-001
Calculate the approximation factor: TurboQuant achieves ≈2.7× optimal
Create table comparing TurboQuant distortion to lower bound for b=1,2,3,4,5
Explain why the gap is smaller at lower bit-widths
Cite all sources with URLs

userStories.title: Set Up CUDA Development Environment
userStories.description: Set up CUDA environment for TurboQuant implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/ directory with CMake build system
CUDA toolkit 12.x configured
cuBLAS and cuRAND linked
Simple test kernel compiles and runs
Google Test or similar for unit testing
Document setup in src/README.md

userStories.title: Implement Random Rotation Matrix Generation
userStories.description: Generate random orthogonal rotation matrices on GPU.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/rotation.cu and src/rotation.cuh
Generate random matrix with i.i.d. N(0,1) entries using cuRAND
Implement QR decomposition for orthogonal matrix (use cuSOLVER or custom)
Unit tests verify: Π·Π^T = I (within numerical tolerance)
Benchmark rotation generation time
Document in src/rotation.md

userStories.title: Implement Codebook Generation
userStories.description: Precompute optimal Lloyd-Max codebooks for Beta distributions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/codebook.cu and src/codebook.cuh
Implement numerical solver for Equation 4 (k-means on Beta distribution)
Generate codebooks for b=1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8 bits
Codebooks match paper values (verify against US-003 research)
Store as compile-time constants or LUT
Handle different dimensions d (codebooks depend on d for finite d)
Document in src/codebook.md

userStories.title: Implement TurboQuant_mse CUDA Kernel
userStories.description: Implement MSE-optimized quantization CUDA kernel.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/turboquant_mse.cu and src/turboquant_mse.cuh
Implement y = Π·x matrix-vector multiply (use cuBLAS)
Implement vectorized nearest-centroid lookup
Implement dequantization x̃ = Π^T·ỹ
Unit tests verify MSE matches theoretical bounds from US-006
Benchmark throughput (vectors/second)
Document in src/turboquant_mse.md

userStories.title: Implement QJL CUDA Kernel
userStories.description: Implement 1-bit QJL transform for residual quantization.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/qjl.cu and src/qjl.cuh
Implement sign(S·r) computation efficiently
Implement dequantization √(π/2)/d · S^T · z
Handle norm storage ||r||
Unit tests verify unbiasedness (average error ≈ 0 over many samples)
Benchmark throughput
Document in src/qjl.md

userStories.title: Implement TurboQuant_prod CUDA Kernel
userStories.description: Implement full inner-product-optimized quantization.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/turboquant_prod.cu and src/turboquant_prod.cuh
Compose TurboQuant_mse (b-1 bits) + QJL (1 bit)
Compute and store residual norm
Implement combined dequantization
Unit tests verify inner product distortion bounds from US-007
Document in src/turboquant_prod.md

userStories.title: Implement Fused Attention Kernel
userStories.description: Create fused attention kernel with TurboQuant KV cache.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/attention_turboquant.cu
Quantize-on-write for K, V vectors
Dequantize-on-read for attention computation
FlashAttention-style memory access patterns
Handle different head dimensions (64, 128, 256)
Benchmark vs baseline attention
Document in src/attention_turboquant.md

userStories.title: Analyze GLM5 KV Cache Architecture
userStories.description: Study the local GLM5 repository to understand its KV cache structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/09-glm5-kv-cache.md
READ the actual code in /home/lily/wsl_git/glm5 - do NOT just web search
Document the directory structure and key files
Find and document KV cache tensor shapes: [batch, heads, seq_len, head_dim]
Identify the attention implementation file(s) with exact paths
Document how K and V are stored, accessed, and evicted
Identify any existing quantization or compression code
Identify exact hook points for TurboQuant integration
Include code snippets from the actual repository
Document model config: num_layers, num_heads, head_dim, etc.
userStories.instructions: Use bash to explore /home/lily/wsl_git/glm5. Read Python files directly. Do NOT rely on web search for code structure.

userStories.title: Analyze DeepSeekV3.2 KV Cache Architecture
userStories.description: Study the local DeepSeekV3.2 repository to understand its KV cache and MLA structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Create research/10-deepseek-kv-cache.md
READ the actual code in /home/lily/wsl_git/deepseekv3_2 - do NOT just web search
Document the directory structure and key files
Document MLA (Multi-head Latent Attention) structure from the actual code
Explain how MLA differs from standard MHA (with code references)
Document existing compression mechanisms in the codebase
Find KV cache implementation with exact file paths
Identify how TurboQuant would complement MLA's latent compression
Include code snippets from the actual repository
Document model config: num_layers, num_heads, head_dim, latent_dim, etc.
userStories.instructions: Use bash to explore /home/lily/wsl_git/deepseekv3_2. Read Python files directly. Do NOT rely on web search for code structure.

userStories.title: Integrate TurboQuant with GLM5
userStories.description: Integrate TurboQuant into GLM5's inference pipeline based on US-016 analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Create integrations/glm5/turboquant_kv.py
Reference the hook points identified in US-016
Hook quantization into KV cache write path
Hook dequantization into attention read path
Handle outlier channels (32 outliers at higher precision)
Test: GLM5 inference runs without crashes
Test: Basic generation produces coherent output
Document integration in integrations/glm5/README.md

userStories.title: Integrate TurboQuant with DeepSeekV3.2
userStories.description: Integrate TurboQuant into DeepSeekV3.2 inference based on US-017 analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Create integrations/deepseek/turboquant_kv.py
Reference the MLA structure documented in US-017
Handle MLA's latent attention (may need to quantize latent vectors differently)
Hook into appropriate cache layers
Test: DeepSeekV3.2 inference runs without crashes
Test: Basic generation produces coherent output
Document integration in integrations/deepseek/README.md

userStories.title: Benchmark Distortion vs Paper
userStories.description: Validate distortion metrics match paper results.
acceptanceCriteria: Create benchmarks/distortion-validation.md
Download or generate test embeddings (e.g., random unit vectors)
Measure MSE for b=1,2,3,4 bits
Measure inner product distortion
Compare to theoretical bounds from US-006, US-007, US-008
Results within 5% of paper values
Generate comparison plots (can use matplotlib)
Document methodology and results

userStories.title: Run Needle-in-Haystack Benchmark
userStories.description: Validate KV cache quantization on the NIAH benchmark.
acceptanceCriteria: Create benchmarks/niah-results.md
Set up NIAH evaluation following paper methodology
Test at context lengths: 4k, 8k, 16k, 32k, 64k (as hardware allows)
Test at 4x compression ratio (4 bits effective)
Target: Score ≥ 0.99 matching paper
Generate comparison plot with baselines if available
Document any discrepancies from paper results

userStories.title: Run LongBench-E Evaluation
userStories.description: Validate on LongBench-E end-to-end generation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create benchmarks/longbench-results.md
Set up LongBench-E evaluation
Test TurboQuant at 2.5-bit and 3.5-bit (with outlier handling)
Measure scores across task categories
Target: Scores within 1% of full precision
Generate comparison table
Document methodology and any issues

userStories.title: Optimize CUDA Kernels for Production
userStories.description: Profile and optimize CUDA implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create benchmarks/performance-optimization.md
Profile with Nsight Compute (or nvprof)
Identify memory bandwidth bottlenecks
Optimize memory access patterns (coalescing)
Reduce register pressure if needed
Document throughput (tokens/second or vectors/second)
Document latency overhead vs unquantized
Verify 4-5x memory savings
List remaining optimization opportunities

userStories.title: Create Documentation and Examples
userStories.description: Create comprehensive documentation for users.
acceptanceCriteria: Complete README.md with quick start guide
Create docs/installation.md with tested instructions
Create docs/api.md documenting public functions
Create examples/glm5_example.py with working code
Create examples/deepseek_example.py with working code
Add docs/troubleshooting.md with common issues
Ensure all code examples actually run
